---
title: "CodeBook Studio"
date: "2026"
date-format: "YYYY"
abstract: "A browser-based annotation tool for computational social science. Define a codebook, annotate texts, and export labelled data for LLM benchmarking in CodeBook Lab."
hide-abstract: true
image: "../images/codebook-studio.png"
applink: "https://codebook.streamlit.app/"
codelink: "https://github.com/LorcanMcLaren/codebook-studio"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
    template-partials:
      - "../partials/title-block.html"
---

CodeBook Studio is a browser-based text annotation tool for computational social science. Researchers define an annotation task once — with sections, instructions, examples, and response formats — annotate texts in the browser, and export the codebook and labelled data for downstream use, including systematic LLM benchmarking in [CodeBook Lab](codebook-lab.qmd).

- Binary, categorical, Likert-scale, and open-ended text annotation types
- Codebook editor with integrated instructions, examples, and live annotation preview
- Consistent annotation interface for teams of coders
- Codebook export in JSON, LaTeX, and Markdown for papers and appendices
- LLM prompt preview for checking how the task will be presented to models
- CSV input and output for interoperability

For a step-by-step walkthrough covering both tools, see the [CodeBook Studio & Lab Tutorial](../codebook-tutorial.qmd).

The app is available at [codebook.streamlit.app](https://codebook.streamlit.app/). Feel free to reach out if you encounter any bugs or need additional features for your annotation workflow.
